title: Short Variable Declaration
description: Declare and initialize variables using the := syntax.
problemStatement:
Go is statically typed, but can infer types! Use the shorthand operator `:=` to declare a variable `name` with `"Gopher"` and `level` with `10`. Print them separated by a space.

**Requirement:** Do not use the `var` keyword.
hints: The `:=` operator is only available inside functions.
It automatically detects the type from the value on the right.
solutionCode:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    name := "Gopher"
    level := 10
    fmt.Println(name, level)
}
